_comment: Things only the user can do (require human accounts, decisions, or external actions). The nightly report surfaces the open ones in Telegram.

items.title: Register terminaljetaime.com on Google Search Console
items.why: Without GSC you can't see real impressions, clicks, or ranking. Free, ~10 min.

items.title: Submit sitemap.xml in Google Search Console
items.why: Tells Google to index the FAQ, About, and category anchors faster.

items.title: Set up Google Alerts for "terminal je t'aime"
items.why: Get an email anytime someone mentions the brand. Helps you reply to community posts and track citation.

items.title: Make the GitHub repo public
items.why: Indexes on Google, listed by 'Awesome' lists, gives the site social proof.

items.title: Draft & launch on Product Hunt (Tue or Wed morning ET)
items.why: Highest single-day traffic spike available to a free dev tool. Free tools do well there.

items.title: Post a "Show HN" on Hacker News (weekday ~9am ET)
items.why: One front-page hit = thousands of devs see it. Format: "Show HN: Terminal Je T'aime – macOS terminal cheat sheet with instant copy".

items.title: Post tailored writeups in r/commandline, r/macOS, r/webdev
items.why: Each sub gets a different angle (no cross-posting). Lead with the value, not the link.

items.title: Submit to DevHunt, free-for.dev, Awesome Terminal, Awesome macOS
items.why: Directory backlinks help both Google and AI engines find the site.

items.title: Add ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to GitHub repo secrets
items.why: Unlocks the nightly workflow to use Claude for autonomous content improvements (new commands, FAQ entries, testimonial polish). Without it, the nightly job only does deterministic freshness updates.

items.title: Add TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID to GitHub repo secrets
items.why: Required so the nightly GitHub Action can send you the Telegram report. (Local .env.local already works.)
